_label_note: SA Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered Secretariat role-mailbox bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, URL CR/NID/Iqama fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording. Generated placeholder/invalid KSA National ID, Iqama, and CR labels were removed from strict must_detect when current deterministic strict mode suppresses them.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Commercial‑term signals: vendor RFP matrices may use red/amber/green flags for benchmarks only; do not infer pricing guidance for any security or sukuk from procurement notes.
must not request full National ID/Iqama—use masked fields

must_detect.matched_text: Commercial‑term signals: vendor RFP matrices may use red/amber/green flags for benchmarks only; do not infer pricing guidance for any security or sukuk from procurement notes.

Purpose: training and operating guidance on PDPL handling, capital-markets conduct hygiene, and issuer workflows for locally listed entities.
PDPL reminder: PII includes any data identifying a natural person directly/indirectly; marketing forms must not request full National ID/Iqama—use masked fields (e.g., Iqama: XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXX-X), and route copies via the PD mailbox pdpl@madarcloud.sa.
Cross-border data: any transfer to non-KSA processors requires SDAIA assessment and a standard transfer register entry; vendor tickets must include “No-Personals” if only hashed telemetry is exported.
CR/onboarding: when collecting commercial registration, accept only business labels (e.g., CR: 7XX-00-12X45), avoid attaching signatory IDs, and store documents in the “Issuer-Workflow/Disclosures/Public” folder unless Legal flags otherwise.
Crypto references in this document are educational (e.g., sandbox “RimalPay Token Pilot” with synthetic wallets: WAL-XX-0000); there is no live trading, custody, or exchange listing activity implied.
ESG/climate content herein is drawn from already-announced 1444H Sustainability Report excerpts and a mock “Net‑Zero Playbook” brochure; no updates to targets are proposed in this note.
Cyber tabletop: use Scenario C (training only)—simulated DDoS against a staging API; incident codes and timestamps are fictitious and should not be circulated as operational alerts.
Information barriers: staff may reference the “Clean Team Charter v2.1” for separations between research and corporate advisory; insider‑list templates must use role aliases (e.g., FIN‑01, SEC‑02) and the mailbox insiderlist@madarcloud.sa.
Blackout and tipping refresher: group‑wide blackout windows are posted on the public IR site; avoid rumor amplification on internal chat; examples in this note are generic and not issuer‑specific.
Commercial‑term signals: vendor RFP matrices may use red/amber/green flags for benchmarks only; do not infer pricing guidance for any security or sukuk from procurement notes.
MNPI (restricted): the Board has received a non‑public cyber‑incident briefing on 28 Sha’ban 1447H regarding temporary degradation in a private client modul
